name: harness-lock
summary: A per-component single-writer WRITE-LOCK enforced as a PreToolUse handler (separate from confinement/). A WRITE (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit/mcp/Bash) to a harness component acquires or refreshes the component's lock under the acting session's identity; a WRITE that would collide with a LIVE lock held by a DIFFERENT holder is blocked. Two agents cannot edit the same component concurrently; git still reconciles at merge, but the collision is prevented locally.

admission.note: Candidate. Built + tested; live PreToolUse wiring in .claude/settings.json is PENDING human wire-in. NOT admitted.

input.description: A Claude Code PreToolUse payload on stdin.

exit: Exits 2 (blocked) when a WRITE targets a component held by a LIVE foreign lock; 0 otherwise (acquiring/refreshing the lock). Fail-open on any guard error.
invariants: A control: fail CLOSED on a real conflict (a live foreign lock → exit 2), fail OPEN on any guard error (bad payload, fs error, unknown state → exit 0).
Holder identity is stable across a session + its subagents (session_id → HARNESS_LOCK_HOLDER → git branch) so a single actor never self-blocks.
A lock is LIVE iff its ts is within TTL (2h) AND its pid is alive; a stale or dead-pid lock is free and re-acquirable.
Non-harness writes and all reads are allowed; an unresolvable component allows (fail-open).
The lock dir state/harness-locks/ is runtime state — gitignored, never committed; orphan locks are swept by the reconciler.
